result.title: Profile
result.description:
Many connectors (especially OAuth-based) act on behalf of a user.
This class represents the user's profile, which is typically retrieved from
the connector's API during the OAuth flow.

Implementation details for different connectors vary, so we can't guarantee
that all fields will be present.

tool.name: github_add_review_to_pr
tool.title: add_review_to_pr
tool.description: Add a review to a GitHub pull request. review is required for REQUEST_CHANGES and COMMENT events.

position.description: The position in the diff where you want to add a review comment. Note this value is not the same as the line number in the file. The position value equals the number of lines down from the first "@@" hunk header in the file you want to add a comment. The line just below the "@@" line is position 1, the next line is position 2, and so on. The position in the diff continues to increase through lines of whitespace and additional hunks until the beginning of a new file.
position.title: Position

tool.name: github_search
tool.title: search
tool.description: Search files within a specific GitHub repository. Provide a plain string query, avoid GitHub query flags such as ``is:pr``. Include keywords that match file names, functions, or error messages. ``repository_name`` or ``org`` can narrow the search scope. Example: ``query="tokenizer bug" repository_name="tiktoken"``. ``topn`` is the number of results to return. No results are returned if the query is empty.

tool.name: notion_fetch
tool.title: fetch
tool.description: Retrieves details about Notion entities by their ID. You can fetch the following types of entities: - Page, i.e. from a <page> block or a <mention-page> mention - Database, i.e. from a <database> block or a <mention-database> mention Use the "fetch" tool when you need to see the details of one or more Notion entities you already know exists and have their IDs. Provide the Notion entities in the `id` parameter. Content for pages that are returned use the enhanced Markdown format, which is a superset of the standard Markdown syntax. See the full spec in the description of the "create-pages" tool. Notion does not currently have a public concept of Data Sources, and only supports Databases. When rendering the response from this tool, assume the database only has one data source and display the details of the data source as the database, removing any mention of "data sources" from the result.

_meta.connector_description: Search and reference your Notion pages

tools.connector_description: Search and reference your Notion pages

tool.name: notion_search
tool.title: search
tool.description: Perform one or more searches over (query_type): - "internal": Perform one or more semantic searches over your entire Notion workspace and connected sources (Slack, Google Drive, Github, Jira, Microsoft Teams, Sharepoint, OneDrive, or Linear). - "users": Perform one or more searches over the Notion users in the current workspace. You can use search when you need to find information which is not already available via other tools. Parameters: - query: the query to search for. This cannot be an empty string. - query_type: only "internal" or "users" is accepted. OPTIONAL - data_source_url: the URL of the database to search in. OPTIONAL When using query, each search query is an AND token match. Meaning, every token in the query is required to be present in order to match. - Search will return documents that contain all of the keywords in the query. - Therefore, queries should be short and keyword-focused (avoid long natural language). - If no results are found, try the following strategies: 1) Use different or related keywords. 2) Make the query more generic and simpler. - To improve recall, consider variants of your terms: abbreviations, synonyms, etc. - Previous search results can provide hints about useful variants of internal terms — use those to refine queries.

_meta.connector_description: Search and reference your Notion pages

tools.connector_description: Search and reference your Notion pages

tool.name: gmail_delete_emails
tool.title: delete_emails
tool.description: Move one or more existing Gmail messages to Trash. This matches Gmail's delete behavior and does not permanently delete the messages.

_meta.connector_description: Find and reference emails from your inbox.

tools.connector_description: Find and reference emails from your inbox.

tool.name: gmail_forward_emails
tool.title: forward_emails
tool.description: Forward one or more existing Gmail messages. Each source message is sent as a separate forwarded email, with the original message inlined below any optional note in the forwarded body and the original attachments preserved on the new outbound email. The note is rendered from Markdown and inserted at the top of each forwarded message. When Gmail thread metadata is available, the sent forward is also kept associated with the original conversation in the sender's mailbox.

note.description: Optional note to place at the top of each forwarded email body. Supports Markdown formatting.
note.title: Note

tool.name: gmail_get_profile

tool.description:
Return the current Gmail user's profile information.
This action may fail because it needs an OAuth permission that was not requested when this connection was created. Reconnect to request the new permission.

result.title: Profile
result.description:
Many connectors (especially OAuth-based) act on behalf of a user.
This class represents the user's profile, which is typically retrieved from
the connector's API during the OAuth flow.

Implementation details for different connectors vary, so we can't guarantee
that all fields will be present.

tool.name: gmail_search_emails
tool.title: search_emails
tool.description: Search Gmail for emails matching a query or label. Place Gmail search query first, followed by search operators (``after:``, ``before:``, ``from:``, ``to:``, ``subject:(words)``, ``has:attachment``). Example: ``project update after:2025-01-01 from:alice@example.com``.

tool.name: google calendar_batch_read_event
tool.title: batch_read_event
tool.description: Read multiple Google Calendar events by ID.

calendar_id.description: Calendar ID to query. Use `primary` for the user's main calendar, or an email-like calendar ID containing `@` (for example `team@group.calendar.google.com`). Default is `primary`.
calendar_id.title: Calendar Id

_meta.connector_description: Look up events and availability.

tools.connector_description: Look up events and availability.

tool.name: google calendar_create_event
tool.title: create_event
tool.description: Create a new Google Calendar event and return its details. If `add_google_meet` is true, Google may return a pending conference state before the Meet link is fully provisioned. Re-read the event later if you need finalized conference details.

minutes.description: Minutes before the event when the reminder triggers.
minutes.title: Minutes

use_default.description: Whether to use the calendar's default reminders for this event.
use_default.title: Use Default

overrides.description: Custom reminder overrides. Provide an empty list with use_default=false to disable reminders for the event.
overrides.title: Overrides

start_time.description: Event start datetime in full ISO-8601/RFC3339 format (e.g. 2026-05-01T09:00:00-07:00).
start_time.title: Start Time

end_time.description: Event end datetime in full ISO-8601/RFC3339 format (e.g. 2026-05-01T10:00:00-07:00).
end_time.title: End Time

attendees.description: List of attendee emails to invite. The authenticated user's attendance is controlled by `self_attendance`. Pass an empty list for a solo status block. For `outOfOffice`, this must be an empty list.
attendees.title: Attendees

timezone_str.description: IANA timezone name such as `America/Los_Angeles` or `Europe/Berlin`. Do not pass UTC offsets like `+02:00`. Default is `America/Los_Angeles`.
timezone_str.title: Timezone Str

reminders.description: Event reminder configuration. Use the calendar defaults when omitted.

event_type.description: Optional event type. Use `outOfOffice` or `focusTime` for status events. For a personal focus block, prefer `attendees=[]`; use `self_attendance="omit"` if you do not want the authenticated user added as an attendee. For `outOfOffice`, Google Calendar rejects events with attendees, so use `attendees=[]` and `self_attendance="omit"`.
event_type.title: Event Type

chat_status.description: Chat status for focus time events
chat_status.title: Chat Status

visibility.description: Optional event visibility (`default`, `public`, or `private`). Leave null for Google default behavior.
visibility.title: Visibility

transparency.description: Optional event transparency. Use `opaque` to block the time as busy, or `transparent` to keep the event from blocking the calendar so overlapping bookings can still be scheduled. Leave null for Google default behavior.
transparency.title: Transparency

add_google_meet.description: If true, request a Google Meet link for the event. If conference creation is still pending, re-read the event later to check final Meet details.
add_google_meet.title: Add Google Meet

recurrence.description: Optional raw Google/RFC5545 recurrence lines (for example `RRULE:FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAY=MO`). Omit for one-off events.
recurrence.title: Recurrence

self_attendance.description: How the authenticated user should be represented on the event they create. Defaults to `accepted`; use `omit` to create the event without adding the authenticated user as an attendee. For `outOfOffice`, this must be `omit`. For a solo `focusTime` block, prefer `omit`.
self_attendance.title: Self Attendance

tool.description: Get details for a single Google Calendar event.

tools.connector_description: Look up events and availability.

tool.name: google calendar_get_availability
tool.title: get_availability
tool.description:
Look up busy windows on one or more calendars before scheduling a meeting. Use this action when the user wants availability for a coworker, room, or other known calendar ID. `time_min` and `time_max` must be full RFC3339 datetimes with `Z` or an explicit UTC offset. `response_timezone_str` controls only how Google formats the busy window timestamps in the response. This action returns busy windows only, not event titles or details, and inaccessible calendars are reported as per-calendar errors.
This action may fail because it needs an OAuth permission that was not requested when this connection was created. Reconnect to request the new permission.

calendar_ids.description: List of calendar IDs to query. Use Google Calendar IDs such as `primary`, a coworker email, or a room/resource email.
calendar_ids.title: Calendar Ids

time_min.description: Required RFC3339 datetime string with `Z` or an explicit UTC offset (for example `2026-05-01T09:00:00-07:00`). Do not pass naive datetimes and do not pass `now`.
time_min.title: Time Min

time_max.description: Required RFC3339 datetime string with `Z` or an explicit UTC offset (for example `2026-05-01T10:00:00-07:00`). Do not pass naive datetimes and do not pass `now`.
time_max.title: Time Max

response_timezone_str.description: Required IANA timezone name used for response timestamps only, such as `America/Los_Angeles` or `Europe/Berlin`. This does not define the query interval.
response_timezone_str.title: Response Timezone Str

the connector's API during the OAuth flow.

that all fields will be present.

event_id.title: Event Id

tool.name: google calendar_search
tool.title: search
tool.description: Search Google Calendar events within an optional time window. Accepted parameters are only `query`, `max_results`, `time_min`, and `time_max`. `query` is broad free text, not a structured search language. Do not pass unsupported fields like `topn`, `timezone_str`, `calendar_id`, `user_message`, or `best_effort_fetch`.

query.description: Broad free-text search over event titles plus some Google-indexed text fields such as descriptions, locations, and some email fields. Reliable for event titles and some email matches, but do not rely on this as a precise attendee-name filter.
query.title: Query

max_results.description: Maximum number of events to return. Must be at least 1.
max_results.title: Max Results

time_min.description: Optional window start in full ISO-8601/RFC3339 format (e.g. 2026-05-01T00:00:00Z).
time_min.title: Time Min

time_max.description: Optional window end in full ISO-8601/RFC3339 format (e.g. 2026-05-31T23:59:59Z).
time_max.title: Time Max

_meta.connector_description: Look up events and availability.

tools.connector_description: Look up events and availability.

tool.name: google calendar_search_events
tool.title: search_events
tool.description: Look up Google Calendar events using various filters. `query` is broad free text, not a structured search language.

time_min.description: Start of the time window. Use full ISO-8601/RFC3339 datetime (for example `2026-05-01T00:00:00Z`) or exact `now`. Do not use relative expressions like `now-7d` or `now+30m`.
time_min.title: Time Min

time_max.description: End of the time window. Use full ISO-8601/RFC3339 datetime (for example `2026-05-31T23:59:59Z`) or exact `now`. Do not use relative expressions like `now-7d` or `now+30m`.
time_max.title: Time Max

timezone_str.description: Timezone for interpreting time_min/time_max. IANA timezone name such as `America/Los_Angeles` or `Europe/Berlin`. Do not pass UTC offsets like `+02:00`. Default is `America/Los_Angeles`.
timezone_str.title: Timezone Str

max_results.description: Maximum number of events to return. Must be at least 1.
max_results.title: Max Results

query.description: Broad free-text search over event titles plus some Google-indexed text fields such as descriptions, locations, and some email fields. Do not rely on this as a precise attendee-name filter.
query.title: Query

calendar_id.description: Calendar ID to query. Use `primary` for the user's main calendar, or an email-like calendar ID containing `@` (for example `team@group.calendar.google.com`). Default is `primary`.
calendar_id.title: Calendar Id

next_page_token.description: Pagination token returned by a previous search_events/search_events_all_fields call. Omit on the first page.
next_page_token.title: Next Page Token

_meta.connector_description: Look up events and availability.

tools.connector_description: Look up events and availability.

tool.name: google calendar_update_event
tool.title: update_event
tool.description: Update an existing Google Calendar event. If `add_google_meet` is true, Google may return a pending conference state before the Meet link is fully provisioned. Re-read the event later if you need finalized conference details.

minutes.description: Minutes before the event when the reminder triggers.
minutes.title: Minutes

use_default.description: Whether to use the calendar's default reminders for this event.
use_default.title: Use Default

overrides.description: Custom reminder overrides. Provide an empty list with use_default=false to disable reminders for the event.
overrides.title: Overrides

event_id.description: Google Calendar event ID, typically from search results.
event_id.title: Event Id

title.description: Title of the event. Leave null to keep the same title.
title.title: Title

start_time.description: New start datetime in full ISO-8601/RFC3339 format. Leave null to keep the same start time.
start_time.title: Start Time

end_time.description: New end datetime in full ISO-8601/RFC3339 format. Leave null to keep the same end time.
end_time.title: End Time

timezone_str.description: IANA timezone name such as `America/Los_Angeles` or `Europe/Berlin`. Do not pass UTC offsets like `+02:00`. Provide this whenever changing start_time and/or end_time.
timezone_str.title: Timezone Str

description.description: Description of the event. Leave null to keep the same description.
description.title: Description

location.description: Location of the event. Leave null to keep the same location.
location.title: Location

reminders.description: Reminder configuration. Omit to keep existing reminders.

attendees_to_add.description: List of attendee email addresses to add to the event. Each entry must be an email address or `me`. Omit or pass an empty list to leave additions unchanged.
attendees_to_add.title: Attendees To Add

attendees_to_remove.description: List of attendee email addresses to remove from the event. Each entry must be an email address or `me`. Omit or pass an empty list to leave removals unchanged.
attendees_to_remove.title: Attendees To Remove

event_type.description: Type for status events (`focusTime`/`outOfOffice`). Google Calendar does not allow changing an existing event's eventType.
event_type.title: Event Type

chat_status.description: Chat status for focus time events
chat_status.title: Chat Status

visibility.description: Optional event visibility (`default`, `public`, or `private`). Leave null to keep existing visibility unchanged.
visibility.title: Visibility

transparency.description: Optional event transparency. Use `opaque` to block the time as busy, or `transparent` to keep the event from blocking the calendar so overlapping bookings can still be scheduled. Leave null to keep existing transparency unchanged.
transparency.title: Transparency

add_google_meet.description: If true, request a Google Meet link for the event. If conference creation is still pending, re-read the event later to check final Meet details.
add_google_meet.title: Add Google Meet

update_scope.description: How to apply this update to a recurring event. Use `this_instance` to update only the selected occurrence, `entire_series` to update the recurring series master and apply the change to the whole recurring series, or `this_and_following` to split the recurring series at the selected occurrence and apply changes from there onward. For non-recurring events, `entire_series` behaves the same as `this_instance`.
update_scope.title: Update Scope

recurrence.description: New raw Google/RFC5545 recurrence lines. Only valid for `entire_series` or `this_and_following`. Omit to leave recurrence unchanged. For `this_and_following`, omitting recurrence reuses the current series rule from the split point onward unless the original rule is COUNT-based.
recurrence.title: Recurrence
